howto.title: How to translate a game
howto.subtitle: Four steps, from opening it to the patch
howto.s1: Open the Library and scan your stores (Steam, Epic, GOG…), or add a game by picking its folder from disk.
howto.s2: Click a game to open its page: GameStringer detects the engine on its own.
howto.s3: Press "Auto-Translate": pick the language and the AI provider (local Ollama is free).
howto.s4: Start it. It extracts the text, translates, and applies the patch — with an automatic backup before touching any file.
howto.note: For classic RPG Maker games (2000/2003) there is no extractable text: an on-screen OCR overlay translates while you play.
howto.cta: Get started: go to the Library

disclaimer.title: Terms of Use
disclaimer.subtitle: Read before continuing
disclaimer.welcomeSubtitle: Before you start, please read carefully
disclaimer.mainDisclaimerText: The developer is not responsible for translations, patches or modifications created with this software.
disclaimer.gameModificationText: GameStringer modifies game files to apply translations. This process is reversible (automatic backup), but carries potential risks.
disclaimer.useAtOwnRiskText: You use this software at your own risk. Always backup before modifying game files.
disclaimer.intellectualPropertyText: Games and trademarks belong to their respective owners. GameStringer is not affiliated with any publisher.
disclaimer.translationQualityText: Automatic translations may contain errors or inaccuracies.
disclaimer.noWarrantyText: Software provided "as is", without warranties of any kind.
disclaimer.communityServerText: GameStringer connects to an external server for Community Hub features: sharing translations, downloading packs, user profiles and interaction with other users. Your profile data and shared translations are transmitted and stored on remote servers. Do not share copyrighted content or sensitive personal information.
disclaimer.dataPrivacyText: Local data (game library, translations, settings) stays on your PC. Only content you choose to share via the Community Hub is sent to the server. AI provider API keys (OpenAI, DeepL, etc.) are stored locally and are never transmitted to our servers.
disclaimer.aiServicesText: When using cloud AI providers (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, DeepL, Lingva), texts to translate are sent to the respective third-party services according to their policies. For maximum privacy, use Ollama for fully local translation.
disclaimer.onlineGamingText: Do not use GameStringer with competitive multiplayer games. File modifiers can trigger anti-cheat systems (VAC, BattlEye, EAC) and cause permanent bans. Use only on single-player or offline games.
disclaimer.aiTranslationText: Translations are generated by Artificial Intelligence (Ollama, OpenAI, etc.). They are not perfect and may contain errors. Quality depends on the chosen model and game context.
disclaimer.privacyText: If you use external AI services (not local Ollama), texts are sent to their servers. Never send personal data or sensitive information through the translator.
disclaimer.legalText: Fan translations are community work. The GameStringer developer is not responsible for translations created by users. Respect the rights of game owners.
disclaimer.agreement: By clicking "Accept and Continue", you confirm you have read and understood these terms. You commit to using GameStringer responsibly and only on games you own for personal use.
disclaimer.summary: By continuing, you accept these terms and are responsible for your use of the software.

v1_8_0.0: Live Translation Overlay: real-time translation via transparent OCR overlay on top of any game — screen capture → multi-engine OCR → AI translation (Groq/Cerebras) → gaming-style overlay. Hotkey Ctrl+Alt+O

v1_8_0.2: Translation Memory Network: federated translation memory network — translators contribute to global pool (opt-in, privacy-first). Next user finds strings already translated. Auto-integrated in translation pipeline
v1_8_0.3: AI Dubbing Pipeline: 7-step pipeline for automatic dubbing — scan audio → Whisper STT → AI translation → TTS voice synthesis with voice matching → duration matching → audio file patching → Rhubarb lip sync → subtitle generation SRT/VTT/ASS
v1_8_0.4: Plugin System: PatcherPlugin interface for community-created patchers — detect/extract/patch/verify/restore lifecycle. Template generator for plugin scaffold. No Rust compilation needed
v1_8_0.5: Security: hardened CSP (removed unsafe-eval), XSS eliminated, AES-256-GCM encrypted API key storage, CSRF protection, Zod validation, global rate limiting
v1_8_0.6: Architecture: CI pipeline with tsc+eslint+vitest+npm audit, 71 new tests, 18 modules extracted from monolithic files, 1197/1203 console.* migrated to structured logger, 893 catch clauses typed
v1_8_0.7: Refactoring: ai-translate-direct.ts (-429 lines, 5 modules), game-detail-client.tsx (-636 lines, 8 components), translator/pro/page.tsx (-776 lines, 5 components)
v1_8_0.8: Dependencies: removed duplicate react-hot-toast and vdf, -42 packages

v1_7_0.2: Gridly CSV Export/Import: multi-language format compatible with Gridly/Lokalise/Crowdin (ID | source | target per language), auto-detect delimiter and language columns
v1_7_0.3: Duration Matching: voice synthesis with automatic speed adaptation to maintain original audio duration — two-pass TTS with Web Audio API measurement
